2605.28070.title: Bridging the Detection-to-Abstention Gap in Reasoning Models under Insufficient Information
2605.28070.paper_summary: We highlight a failure mode of large reasoning models on questions with insufficient information: models may recognize that a problem is under-specified, yet still continue reasoning and produce unsupported final answers instead of abstaining. We formalize this mismatch as the detection-to-abstention gap, where detected insufficiency fails to translate into final abstention. This gap is especially concerning in high-risk domains such as medical AI, where answers based on incomplete evidence can be more harmful than refusal. To close this gap, we propose Judge-Then-Solve (JTS), a trajectory-level reasoning-control framework that trains models to make an explicit answerability commitment before solution generation. Rather than treating abstention as a final-answer style, JTS casts it as a control decision: the model either proceeds to solve or terminates early based on its answerability judgment. We instantiate this policy through supervised warm-up and missing-premise reinforcement learning with consistency and length-shaping rewards. Experiments on dense and MoE reasoning models show that JTS substantially improves reliable abstention across datasets and pushes Abstention@Detection (A@D) to near-saturation, indicating that models not only detect missing information but also act on that detection. By terminating unanswerable trajectories immediately after the answerability judgment, JTS reduces unnecessary reasoning and improves inference efficiency when continued deliberation would amplify unsupported assumptions. We also observe that missing-premise training can alter reasoning behavior on difficult but answerable problems, reducing unproductive self-reflection. These results suggest that abstention under insufficient information is a key form of reasoning control for deploying reasoning models safely and efficiently.
2605.28070.paper_summary_zh: 我們強調大型推理模型在資訊不足的問題上出現的失效模式：模型可能會識別出問題是未充分指定的，但仍然繼續推理並產生不支持的最終答案，而不是選擇不作答。我們將這種不匹配形式化為檢測到不充分性與不作答之間的差距，這裡檢測到的不充分性未能轉化為最終的不作答。這一差距在高風險領域（如醫療人工智慧）中特別令人擔憂，因為基於不完整證據的答案可能比拒絕回答更具危害性。為了縮小這一差距，我們提出了 Judge-Then-Solve (JTS)，這是一個軌跡級別的推理控制框架，旨在訓練模型在生成解決方案之前做出明確的回答承諾。JTS 將不作答視為一種控制決策，而不是最終答案的風格：模型根據其回答能力的判斷，要麼繼續解決問題，要麼提前終止。我們通過有監督的熱身和缺失前提的強化學習，並結合一致性和長度塑形獎勵來實現這一政策。在密集型和 MoE 推理模型上的實驗顯示，JTS 顯著提高了跨數據集的可靠不作答，並將不作答@檢測（A@D）推向近乎飽和，這表明模型不僅檢測到缺失的信息，還對該檢測做出行動。通過在回答能力判斷後立即終止無法回答的軌跡，JTS 減少了不必要的推理，並提高了推理效率，當持續的深思熟慮會放大不支持的假設時。我們還觀察到，缺失前提的訓練可以改變在困難但可回答問題上的推理行為，減少不生產性的自我反思。這些結果表明，在資訊不足的情況下不作答是安全高效部署推理模型的一種關鍵推理控制形式。
2605.28070.author: Renjie Gu et.al.
2605.28070.authors: Renjie Gu, Jiaxu Li, Yihao Wang, Yun Yue, Hansong Xiao, Yefei Chen, Yuan Wang, Chunxiao Guo, Pei Wei, Jinjie Gu, Yixin Cao
